req.title: Wire transfer (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall initiate a wire transfer requiring two-factor authentication; transfers above $100,000 require manual review. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has four distinct testable behaviours: (1) happy-path wire under $100k queued immediately, (2) wire at/above $100k triggers manual-review 202, (3) stale 2FA returns 401, and (4) duplicate Idempotency-Key returns the original result. EP covers the normal under-threshold case; BVA pins the $100,000 boundary; NEG covers the stale-2FA auth failure; STC models the idempotency state transition from first-submission to duplicate-submission; DT covers the combined auth+amount decision table for the manual-review path. Tests use a shared fixture for HTTP session setup and a helper for fresh idempotency keys. Each test is self-contained and resets state via unique keys or mocked preconditions.

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_under_threshold_queued
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=50000, valid auth token, and unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: compliance_check field indicates check has started
trace: R-FIN-081: wire queued on valid submission under threshold
R-FIN-081: compliance check started on queued wire
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_under_threshold_queued(api_session, fresh_idempotency_key, valid_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "amount": 50000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "routing_number": "<test_routing_number>"
    }
    headers = {**valid_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": fresh_idempotency_key}
    resp = api_session.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "queued"
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body.get("compliance_check") in ("started", "pending")

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_stale_2fa_returns_401
preconditions: Customer 2FA was verified MORE than 5 minutes ago (stale token)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=10000 and a stale/expired 2FA auth token
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating stale or expired 2FA
No transfer_id is present in the response
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_stale_2fa_returns_401(api_session, fresh_idempotency_key, stale_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "amount": 10000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "routing_number": "<test_routing_number>"
    }
    headers = {**stale_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": fresh_idempotency_key}
    resp = api_session.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" not in body
    error = body.get("error", "").lower()
    assert any(kw in error for kw in ("2fa", "authentication", "expired", "stale"))

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_at_100k_threshold_requires_manual_review
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=100000, valid auth token, and unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: HTTP 202 status code indicating manual review required
Response body contains status indicating pending manual review
trace: R-FIN-081: transfers above $100,000 require manual review
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_at_100k_threshold_requires_manual_review(api_session, fresh_idempotency_key, valid_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "amount": 100000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "routing_number": "<test_routing_number>"
    }
    headers = {**valid_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": fresh_idempotency_key}
    resp = api_session.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    status_val = body.get("status", "").lower()
    assert any(kw in status_val for kw in ("manual_review", "pending_review", "review"))

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_idempotency_duplicate_returns_original
preconditions: A fixed Idempotency-Key is used for both submissions
First submission has already been accepted by the system
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=20000, valid auth token, and a fixed Idempotency-Key (first submission)
Record the transfer_id and status from the first response
POST /transfers/wire again with identical payload and the SAME Idempotency-Key (duplicate submission)
Compare the second response against the first
expected: Both submissions return the same HTTP status code
Both responses contain the same transfer_id
Both responses contain the same status value
No new transfer is created on the duplicate submission
trace: R-FIN-081: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return the original result
STC: state transition from initial-submission to duplicate-submission
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_idempotency_duplicate_returns_original(api_session, fixed_idempotency_key, valid_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "amount": 20000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "routing_number": "<test_routing_number>"
    }
    headers = {**valid_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": fixed_idempotency_key}
    resp1 = api_session.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code in (200, 201, 202)
    body1 = resp1.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body1
    resp2 = api_session.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert resp2.status_code == resp1.status_code
    assert body2.get("transfer_id") == body1.get("transfer_id")
    assert body2.get("status") == body1.get("status")

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_above_threshold_with_valid_2fa_requires_review
preconditions: Customer 2FA verified within last 5 minutes (valid auth)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=150000, valid auth token, and unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP status code, transfer_id, and compliance fields
expected: Response body status indicates manual review pending
trace: R-FIN-081: transfers above $100,000 require manual review
DT: decision rule - valid 2FA AND amount > $100k => 202 + manual review + compliance check
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_above_threshold_with_valid_2fa_requires_review(api_session, fresh_idempotency_key, valid_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "amount": 150000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "routing_number": "<test_routing_number>"
    }
    headers = {**valid_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": fresh_idempotency_key}
    resp = api_session.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    status_val = body.get("status", "").lower()
    assert any(kw in status_val for kw in ("manual_review", "pending_review", "review"))
    assert body.get("compliance_check") in ("started", "pending")
